data.content:
The build confirms CS0246 — the generated file is written but never added to compilation. MSBuild's implicit globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the file generated during the build is invisible to Csc.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to the `Compile` item group inside the target, and register it with `FileWrites` for clean support.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild's implicit globbing happens at evaluation time (before any targets run), so the generated file isn't picked up.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, and also register it with `FileWrites` for clean support.

This is the classic "including-generated-files" pattern. Let me fix it.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
**Root cause:** MSBuild's implicit file globs (`<Compile>`) expand at **evaluation time**, before any targets execute. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file during **execution time**, so it's never picked up by the compiler.

**Fix applied** — added two lines inside the target:
- `<Compile Include="...">` — explicitly adds the generated file to the compilation inputs
- `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it so `dotnet clean` removes it properly
